User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the FinancioAI application.

createdAt.description: Date and time when the user account was created.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer of the user's business.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Represents an invoice issued to a customer.

issueDate.description: Date when the invoice was issued.

dueDate.description: Date when the invoice is due.

status.description: Status of the invoice (e.g., sent, paid, overdue).

bankDetails.description: Bank details where the invoice payment should be sent

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents an income or expense transaction.

category.description: Category of the transaction (e.g., rent, salary, utilities).

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Accessible only by the user themselves.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to ensure data isolation and ownership based on user IDs, enabling secure and scalable access. Each user's data (customers, invoices, and transactions) is stored under their respective user ID, which simplifies security rules and improves query performance. This approach ensures that users can only access data that belongs to them.  The use of path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/...`) eliminates the need for complex `get()` calls in security rules, achieving Authorization Independence. This structure supports the QAPs by using Structural Segregation, ensuring that all documents within a user's subcollections share the same security requirements.  There is no mixing of data with different access needs within the same collection. Each top-level collection represents a distinct entity type with consistent access control.
